methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
ideaForge is a public-stage company in the Drones & Autonomous sector that has raised $50M+. The valuation landscape for this company is constrained by a limited peer set: the sector contains only six private comparables, with no public trading comps available. The identifiable private peers include Joby Aviation, Archer Aviation, Zipline, Nuro, and Elroy Air, though none form a sufficiently dense cluster around ideaForge's stage or valuation tier.

The peer-median triangulation method is inapplicable here. A narrow filter requiring same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints—the minimum threshold for reliable median-based valuation. While five same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, they do not meet clustering criteria. An all-sector median approach would introduce substantial noise and misrepresent the dynamics of this specialized frontier-tech market.

Consequently, this analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range derived from comparable-company methods. The core uncertainty is the severe sparsity of the peer universe: ideaForge operates in a nascent sector where most competitors remain private and at disparate stages of development. Meaningful valuation work would require either direct financial modeling tied to unit economics and market-sizing assumptions, or patient accumulation of additional transaction data as the sector matures. At present, any range would rest on assumptions rather than observable market signals.
